computation_name: Semantic Ontology and Taxonomic Similarity Join
definition: Exploit ontology structure, taxonomic prefixes, semantic-overlap filters, information-content bounds, or knowledge-graph path summaries to avoid all-pairs semantic comparison over annotated objects. Verify or rerank candidates with the intended ontology or taxonomic similarity.

scale_profile.best_for: Biomedical, ecological, phenotype, and knowledge-graph audits where annotations are sets of ontology or taxonomy terms and exact semantic comparison is expensive.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Large annotation tables when the ontology graph and feature frequencies can be indexed.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Annotations are flat tokens and ordinary Jaccard or weighted MinHash is the declared scientific similarity.

tunable_parameters.name: semantic_measure
tunable_parameters.purpose: Selects overlap, path, information-content, earth-mover, or graph-based semantic similarity.

tunable_parameters.name: candidate_filter
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls taxonomic prefixes, ancestor filters, semantic overlap bounds, or graph-neighborhood constraints.

tunable_parameters.name: top_k_or_threshold
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls output sparsity and verification work.

quality_gates: Report ontology version and whether ancestors, evidence codes, and information-content weights were used.
Check exact semantic scores on a subset of pruned pairs to estimate false negatives.
Verify that high-level ancestor terms do not dominate similarity for sparsely annotated samples.
failure_modes: Ontology updates can change pairwise similarities and audit reproducibility.
Generic ancestor overlap can make broad annotations look spuriously similar.
Semantic filters may miss distant but meaningful graph-path relationships.
example_usage: Use weighted_jaccard_topk.py after expanding ontology terms to weighted ancestor features as a simple prototype; production systems should preserve the declared semantic measure in the output metadata.

citations.title: KOIOS: Top-k Semantic Overlap Set Search
citations.evidence: Uses filters to efficiently find top-k semantically similar sets under semantic overlap.

citations.title: Efficient Taxonomic Similarity Joins with Adaptive Overlap Constraint
citations.evidence: Uses taxonomic knowledge and adaptive prefix filtering to prune semantic similarity joins.

citations.title: Finding top-k similar pairs of objects annotated with terms from an ontology
citations.evidence: Studies efficient top-k similarity search for ontology-annotated objects under multiple semantic distance measures.
